title: strychnos retinervis
intro: Strychnos retinervis, also known as the reticulated strangler fig, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Strychnos retinervis lie in its roots, which contain strychnine and other alkaloids, making it a potential source for the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat malaria, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Strychnos retinervis typically involve the extraction of its roots, which can be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
